entry.title: Re: Need to fetch value for custom field in Manage Custom...
entry.content:
Hi sappolar,

Short answer: Yes, it is possible — your approach (custom field in business context Accounting: Journal Entry Item + custom logic on FIN_ACDOC_SUBSTITUTION) is exactly the SAP-documented way to show the profit center in Manage Customer Line Items. The most common reason for empty values is that the logic only fills items posted or changed after the logic was published — it does not update existing open items retroactively.

Why the standard Profit Center field is empty

This is standard behavior, not an error. The profit center is written to the G/L account line items only, not to the customer/supplier line item, and one document can contain several G/L items with different profit centers — so no single value exists for the AR line item, and Manage Customer Line Items can only display fields with a one-to-one relation.
Reference: KBA 2901005

Is the custom field + FIN_ACDOC_SUBSTITUTION approach valid?

Yes. As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, you can create a custom field in the Accounting: Journal Entry Item business context and enable its usage in the Manage Customer Line Items app. SAP's own use-case blog shows exactly your scenario: an implementation of the Journal Entry Substitution (FIN_ACDOC_SUBSTITUTION) extension point that copies the profit center from the G/L line item into the custom field displayed in Manage Customer Line Items.
Reference: SAP blog – Custom fields for the Manage Customer Line Items app: use case and announcement blog (2402) and KBA 3405214

Why your values are not coming:

Most likely: you are looking at documents posted before the logic was published. The custom logic is applied to new and changed items only; to test it, select an existing line item, use the Edit Line Item function, and change the item — after saving, the field should be filled for that item.

Constraints to verify:

  • Field enablement: The field usage must be enabled (Enable Usage) for the Manage Customer Line Items app on the User Interface tab in the Custom Fields app, and the field must be published; only then is it available in the app's OData service and Settings dialog.
  • Business transaction coverage: FIN_ACDOC_SUBSTITUTION only works for direct accounting journal entries where BSEG is created; it is not called for CO-generated postings (e.g., settlement). Reference: KBA 3014271. It is also not supported in Manage Automatic Payments (KBA 2890482), and journal entry item substitution does not work for parked invoices — Reference: KBA 3328448.
  • Do not try to substitute the standard Profit Center field itself on the AR line: the profit center cannot be set manually (or via substitution) on customer/supplier line items, and substituting an account assignment that is a document splitting characteristic must be avoided (KBA 2810442). Copying the value into a custom field, as you did, is the supported pattern.

Alternative without extensibility

Use the Display Line Items in General Ledger app: select by Customer ID (optionally filtered by reconciliation account) to list AR line items including their profit center assignments.
Reference: SAP blog – How to Display Profit Center Assignments for AR/AP Line Items

Summary

Your design is correct and supported. Verify the field is enabled and published for the Manage Customer Line Items app, then test by posting a new customer invoice (direct FI posting) or editing an existing line item — historical items will remain empty because the substitution is not applied retroactively. For historical data, use Display Line Items in General Ledger instead.

Hope it helps.

Best regards,

Michal

entry.title: Top Tips & Tricks for SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
entry.content:
Success with SAP Analytics Cloud depends on balancing performance, usability, and governance. By designing efficient data models, simplifying dashboards, leveraging AI-powered insights, securing data appropriately, and standardizing development practices, organizations can build scalable analytics solutions that deliver faster insights and a better user experience. Following these best practices helps reduce maintenance effort, improve report performance, and increase user adoption across the business:

Start with a Clear Data Model

A well-designed data model is the foundation of every successful SAC implementation.

Tips:

  • Remove unnecessary dimensions and measures.
  • Use meaningful names for dimensions and calculations.
  • Create hierarchies for easier navigation.
  • Minimize calculated measures when they can be created in the source system.

Benefit: Faster reports and easier maintenance.

Use Input Controls Effectively

Instead of creating multiple versions of the same story:

  • Add input controls for dimensions like Region, Product, or Year.
  • Allow users to filter dashboards themselves.
  • Link multiple charts to a single input control.

Benefit: Cleaner dashboards with better user experience.

Optimize Story Performance

Large stories can become slow.

Best practices include:

  • Limit the number of charts on a page.
  • Avoid displaying excessive data.
  • Use lazy loading where available.
  • Reduce the number of widgets that refresh simultaneously.

Benefit: Faster dashboard loading times.

Master Bookmarks

Bookmarks allow users to save personalized dashboard views.

Use bookmarks to:

  • Save favorite filters.
  • Preserve chart layouts.
  • Quickly switch between business scenarios.

Benefit: Improved productivity for recurring analysis.

Create Reusable Calculations

Instead of recreating formulas repeatedly:

  • Use calculated measures.
  • Create restricted measures.
  • Use reusable calculated dimensions.

Benefit: Consistent calculations across reports

Use Conditional Formatting

Highlight important information automatically, by using an accepted color schema:

Example:

  • Revenue below target → Red
  • Revenue on target → Yellow
  • Revenue above target → Green

Users immediately identify trends without reading every number.

 Keep Dashboards Simple

A common mistake is overcrowding dashboards.

Recommended guidelines:

  • Focus on one business question per page.
  • Use white space effectively.
  • Limit colors.
  • Avoid unnecessary visualizations.

Simple dashboards are easier to understand and maintain.

Use Templates

Instead of creating stories from scratch:

  • Build corporate templates.
  • Standardize colors.
  • Use consistent fonts.
  • Create reusable page layouts.

Benefit: Professional-looking reports with reduced development time.

Schedule Publications

Automate report distribution.

Examples:

  • Weekly executive dashboards
  • Monthly sales reports
  • Daily KPI summaries

Automatic scheduling saves manual effort and ensures stakeholders receive up-to-date information.

Secure Data with Roles

Always implement role-based security.

Examples:

  • Sales managers view only their region.
  • HR users access employee data.
  • Finance teams view financial models only.

Good security improves governance and compliance.

Use Data Actions for Planning

Planning users can automate repetitive tasks.

Examples:

  • Currency conversion
  • Data allocation
  • Copy versions
  • Forecast calculations

This reduces manual work and improves planning accuracy.

Monitor Performance Regularly

Regularly review:

  • Slow-running stories
  • Large models
  • Complex calculations
  • Frequently accessed dashboards

Continuous optimization prevents performance degradation as data grows.

Make Dashboards Mobile-Friendly

Many executives access reports on tablets or phones.

Design with:

  • Responsive layouts
  • Larger fonts
  • Touch-friendly filters
  • Simplified charts

This improves accessibility across devices.

Document Your Work

Maintain documentation for:

  • Models
  • Calculations
  • Data sources
  • Business rules
  • Naming conventions

Good documentation simplifies onboarding and future enhancements.

Productivity Tricks

  • Use keyboard shortcuts to speed up navigation.
  • Group related widgets for easier page management.
  • Hide technical dimensions that users don't need.
  • Use meaningful chart titles that answer business questions.
  • Apply consistent color coding across all dashboards.
  • Test reports with real business users before publishing.
  • Archive unused stories to keep the environment organized.
  • Validate calculations against source systems before release.

Common Mistakes to Avoid

  • Creating overly complex dashboards.
  • Using too many calculated measures.
  • Ignoring data model optimization.
  • Displaying excessive detail on a single page.
  • Inconsistent naming conventions.
  • Skipping security configuration.
  • Not testing performance with large datasets.
  • Failing to document models and stories.

entry.title: Take Control of External Workforce Rates with SAP Fieldglass
entry.content:
Managing external workforce costs becomes challenging when rates vary across suppliers, locations, and roles. Without clear controls, organizations can face higher costs, inconsistent pricing, and limited visibility into spending.

A structured rate management approach in SAP Fieldglass helps bring consistency to workforce pricing through rate cards, approved supplier rates, and automated workflows. This reduces rate leakage, improves compliance, and makes savings easier to track over time.

Regular benchmarking, supplier comparisons, and spending analytics also help uncover pricing gaps and identify opportunities to improve cost efficiency.

To access Value Map Groups, you need to login to the SAP Community using an S-user ID or a Universal ID. Learn more and explore full document on the Spend Management Value Map, which highlights how sustainable savings come from embedding rate control into the hiring process, supported by governance, automation, and continuous monitoring. 

Also, discover the SAP Ariba & SAP Fieldglass Spend Management value packages which offer a structured and outcome‑driven approach that connects common business challenges to capabilities across SAP’s spend management portfolio.

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps  

Beyond improving supplier compliance, SAP Enterprise Support value maps on SAP Community provides guidance, recommended actions, and supporting resources to help you decide what to focus on next—all included in your SAP cloud subscription.   

👉Watch short demo video, then head over to our page - explore our range of topics and request a call with a topic expert for guidance tailored to your goals.   

About the author:  

Rachana is a Topic Expert for SAP Enterprise Support value maps, specializing in Spend Management. With over 15 years at SAP, she brings deep experience from a range of customer-facing roles. She enjoys working closely with customers to help them make the most of SAP Enterprise Support and drive real results.  

💡Have questions or feedback around this content? Don’t hesitate to comment down below or contact @Rachana_Rasania

entry.title: I am unable to fetch data from anlc table
entry.content:
Hello Guys,

I am trying to fetch data from the ANLC table in a CDS view. However, after the migration from ECC to S/4HANA, I am unable to retrieve any data from ANLC.

As per SAP documentation, ANLC and ANLP have been made obsolete (or replaced by compatibility views), and SAP recommends using the replacement object FAA_ANLC for data selection.

I wrote the CDS query using the replacement object, but it is still not returning the expected results. The same logic was working earlier, but now it is no longer working.

Could someone please help me understand what I might be missing? Is there any additional configuration, authorization, or S/4HANA-specific consideration that I should check?

 

My CDS code is given below.

 

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@EndUserText.label: 'Consumption View for Assets Master'

@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true

define view entity ZC_MM_ASSETS_REPORT

as select from anla as aa

left outer join anlz as az on az.bukrs = aa.bukrs

and az.anln1 = aa.anln1

and az.anln2 = aa.anln2

left outer join Faa_Anlc as ac1 on ac1.bukrs = aa.bukrs

and ac1.anln1 = az.anln1

and ac1.anln2 = az.anln2

and ac1.afabe = '01'

and ac1.gjahr = aa.zujhr

left outer join Faa_Anlc as ac2 on ac2.bukrs = aa.bukrs

and ac2.anln1 = az.anln1

and ac2.anln2 = az.anln2

and ac2.afabe = '01'

and ac2.gjahr = '2025'

//left outer join Faa_Anlp as ap on ap.bukrs = aa.bukrs

// and ap.anln1 = aa.anln1

// and ap.anln2 = aa.anln2

left outer join anlb as ab on ab.bukrs = aa.bukrs

and ab.anln1 = aa.anln1

and ab.anln2 = aa.anln2

and ab.afabe = '01'

left outer join anlu as au on au.bukrs = aa.bukrs

and au.anln1 = aa.anln1

and au.anln2 = aa.anln2

left outer join t001 as t on t.bukrs = ac1.bukrs

{

key aa.bukrs,

key aa.anln1,

key aa.anln2,

aa.anlkl,

aa.zugdt,

aa.aktiv,

aa.deakt,

aa.meins,

@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'MEINS'

aa.menge,

aa.txt50,

aa.txa50,

 

az.kostl,

az.gsber,

 

ac1.gjahr,

ac1.afabe,

@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'WAERS'

ac1.answl,

@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'WAERS'

ac2.knafa,

@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'WAERS'

(ac1.answl + ac2.knafa) as NET_VALUE,

 

//@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'WAERS'

//ap.nafaz,

 

ab.ndjar,

ab.ndper,

 

t.waers,

 

au.zztag_number

}

where aa.anln1 = '001000001450'

entry.title: What is planned for the 2608 release of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing?
entry.content:
The 2608 release of SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing is planned to be deployed to BTP customer tenants on July 22, 2026:

Administration UI and Administration service:

Release

Test/Non-Productive & Productive Upgrades

2608

July 22, 2026

Variant Configuration service & Pricing service:

Release

Test/Non-productive Upgrade

Productive Upgrade

2608

July 22, 2026

August 12, 2026

This blog covers the following planned innovations:

  • Configuration and Pricing Services – New Data Center: Azure US West (Washington) (cf-us20)
  • Configuration and Pricing Services – Extension Concept - Cache Database Queries in Local Extensions
  • Configuration and Pricing Services - OpenAPI Specification Upgrade
  • Pricing service – Extension Concept – Custom Formula Execution During Group Condition Processing
  • Pricing service – Expose Scale Base Information and Group Condition Indicator
  • Pricing service – ReadOnly Changed Document Items
  • Variant Configuration service – New AVC-Aligned Mode (Beta)
  • Variant Configuration service – Additional Filter Attribute for Field Knowledge-base Schema
  • Variant Configuration service – Class Node Specialization Improvement
  • Administration and Data Replication – Modern Administration User Interface

It also includes a call to act regarding the following changes:

  • Configuration and Pricing Services – Extension Concept - Cache Database Queries in Local Extensions
  • Configuration and Pricing Services - OpenAPI Specification Upgrade

Please check the updated Roadmap and Release Notes after the release date to see which of these features were delivered as planned.

Disclaimer

The information in this presentation is confidential and proprietary to SAP and may not be disclosed without the permission of SAP.
Except for your obligation to protect confidential information, this presentation is not subject to your license agreement or any other service or subscription agreement with SAP. SAP has no obligation to pursue any course of business outlined in this presentation or any related document, or to develop or release any functionality mentioned therein.
This presentation, or any related document and SAP's strategy and possible future developments, products and or platforms directions and functionality are all subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice. The information in this presentation is not a commitment, promise or legal obligation to deliver any material, code or functionality. This presentation is provided without a warranty of any kind.

 

Configuration and Pricing Services – New Data Center: Azure US West (Washington) (cf-us20)

SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing continues to expand its global availability with the introduction of a new Azure data center in US West (Washington) (cf-us20).

This new option allows customers with new contracts to deploy their tenants in an additional Microsoft Azure region, complementing the existing global footprint across AWS and Azure regions.

Key aspects to consider:

  • All tenants of a customer must run in the same data center.
  • Mixing infrastructure providers is not supported.
  • Existing tenants cannot be moved across regions or providers.

This ensures operational consistency while offering increased flexibility for new implementations.

 

Configuration and Pricing Services – Extension Concept - Cache Database Queries in Local Extensions

Local extensions can read data from custom database tables via sap.db().execute(). In scenarios where the same extension is called repeatedly — for example across multiple pricing items in a single document — or where several different extensions query the same table, the same SQL statement used to be executed against the database each time, even when the underlying data had not changed. This caused unnecessary database load and added latency to pricing and configuration requests.

With this release, the results of sap.db().execute() calls are automatically cached at the SQL-statement level. The cache deduplicates identical queries within a single request as well as across requests, so the database is only queried once for a given SQL statement and parameter combination within the cache's eviction period of 60 minutes.

Note: With that, cached results may remain outdated for up to 60 minutes after changes occur in the underlying database table.

Action: Check your local extension implementations for database accesses and create a support ticket on component LOD-CPS if you require the caching to be disabled for your tenants.

Example use case:

Consider an extension that looks up a discount rate from a custom table ZDISCOUNT_RATES based on the customer's country. A sales order contains 20 items for different products — so each item carries a different product material number in its pricing attributes. Because the extension input differs per item, the requirement formula is executed for each item and not retrieved from the extension result cache. But as the SQL query which is executed by requirement formula 900 only depends on the country — which is the same for all items in the document — the SQL query is only executed for the first item and retrieved from the SQL query cache for all subsequent items.

function REQ_900(pricingInput) {
    var json = JSON.parse(pricingInput);
    var response;
    if (json.action === 'COLLECT_ATTRIBUTES') {
        response = req_900_collect_attributes();
    }
    if (json.action === 'PROCESS_FORMULA') {
        response = req_900_process_formula(json);
    }
    return JSON.stringify(response);
}

function req_900_process_formula(json) {
    var db = sap.db();
    var country = getAttributeValue(json.documentInput.itemInput.attributes, "KOMK-LAND1");
    // Query depends only on country — SQL query cache hit for all items after the first.
    var select = db.select()
        .columns("DISCOUNT_PCT")
        .from("ZDISCOUNT_RATES")
        .where(db.eq("COUNTRY", db.string(country)))
        .build();
    var dbResult = db.execute(select);
    var discountApplies = dbResult.getRowCount() > 0;
    return { result: discountApplies };
}

The SQL query cache is active by default and requires no changes to existing extension code. If real-time data accuracy is required — for example if an extension queries data that changes frequently — the cache deactivation can be requested from SAP via ticket on component LOD-CPS.

More information will be published with the July release in the Extension Guide (Pre‑Production).

 

Configuration and Pricing Services - OpenAPI Specification Upgrade

As part of our upcoming release, we will upgrade our OpenAPI specification from version 3.0.1 to 3.1.0.

For most customers, this change is expected to have minimal impact on existing integrations. Your current API calls and workflows will continue to function as they do today.

This update focuses on improving how our API is described, by aligning with the latest industry standard (OpenAPI 3.1.0, which is based on JSON Schema Draft 2020-12). It does not introduce changes to API endpoints or behavior.

However, if you use tools that automatically process our API specification, such as code generators, you may need to ensure they support OpenAPI 3.1.0.

Action: Check whether your code generators support OpenAPI 3.1.0 and upgrade if needed

New API specifications will be published with the August release on SAP Business Accelerator Hub for Variant Configuration service, Pricing service, and Administration service.

 

Pricing service – Extension Concept – Custom Formula Execution During Group Condition Processing

Custom pricing logic becomes significantly more powerful with support for formula execution during group condition processing.

Previously limited to item-level processing, value formulas (VAL) and scale base formulas (SCL) can now also be executed during group-level evaluation.

A new field groupProcessing is introduced:

  • true: formula is executed during group condition processing
  • omitted: formula is executed at item level

groupProcessing flag in extension payload

This enhancement allows:

  • More flexible pricing logic
  • Clear separation between item and group-level processing
  • Better alignment with complex pricing scenarios

Activation is controlled on a per-tenant basis via SAP support.

More information will be published with the August release in the API Definition, Development Guide, and Extension Guide (Pre-Production).

 

Pricing service – Expose Scale Base Information and Group Condition Indicator

To improve transparency of pricing results, two new response fields are introduced:

  • scaleBase: provides the calculated scale base value and unit
  • isGroupCondition: indicates whether a condition is a group condition

new fields in pricing response structure

These enhancements enable:

  • Improved UI visualization of pricing results
  • Better traceability and debugging
  • More accurate integration with downstream systems

More information will be published with the August release in the API Definition and Development Guide (Pre‑Production).

 

Pricing service – Read Only Changed Document Items

A new filtering capability enables retrieving only items affected by the most recent operation in a pricing document.

new items-changed filter

Example: GET /pricing/documents/{documentId}?$filter=items-changed eq true

The response returns:

  • Only items that were impacted directly or indirectly
  • A flat list independent of hierarchy

This provides:

  • Better performance through reduced payload
  • Simplified integration for change-based workflows
  • Improved efficiency for incremental updates

More information will be published with the August release in the API Definition and Development Guide (Pre‑Production).

 

Variant Configuration service – New AVC-Aligned Mode (Beta)

A new AVC‑aligned engine mode is introduced for beta testing in non‑productive environments. This innovation is a major step forward in enabling the native processing of Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC) models directly within the Variant Configuration service.

What’s new?

With this release, customers can execute AVC-based product models without relying on AVC Forwarding, by leveraging:

  • A new knowledge-base schema
  • A dedicated AVC-aligned processing mode in the service

new schema 5 and new flag for AVC-Aligned Mode to use the new mode for this KB even with AVC Forwarding enabled

A key innovation is the introduction of service-side constraint compilation:

  • Constraints written with supported syntax are now parsed and compiled directly within the service using an integrated compiler
  • This enables AVC‑like constraint evaluation without dependency on backend execution
  • Classical dependencies such as procedures, preconditions, and selection conditions continue to be compiled in the back end and are delivered as part of the knowledge base

In addition, the following behavior is now aligned with SAP S/4HANA for advanced variant configuration (AVC):

  • All characteristics are treated as restrictable, independent of modeling flags
  • Full alignment with AVC inference logic and domain restriction behavior
  • Introduction of set-based semantics for multivalue characteristics, ensuring more consistent and predictable evaluation results
  • AVC syntax round() is supported in constraints

Business value

This innovation has significant benefits in both architecture and runtime behavior:

Reduced architectural complexity

  • Eliminates the need for AVC Forwarding and cross-engine communication
  • Enables direct execution within the Variant Configuration service

Improved performance and scalability

  • Faster configuration processing through service-side compilation
  • Optimized constraint evaluation and execution
  • Reduced dependency on back-end systems

Lower resource consumption

  • Reduced load on SAP S/4HANA by avoiding forwarding-related processing
  • More efficient use of system resources

Improved functional alignment

  • Consistent behavior across constraint evaluation, inference logic, and characteristic restriction
  • More predictable and transparent configuration results

Seamless adoption

  • No changes required to existing product models or user workflows
  • Unsupported syntax is handled gracefully with warnings instead of runtime errors

Availability note

This innovation is released with restricted availability as part of an initial beta program:

  • Available only for selected pilot customers that are using AVC in S/4HANA on-premise or private cloud, model in a VC-compliant way, and use the Variant Configuration service with such an AVC model without AVC Forwarding
  • Controlled enablement via tenant settings and knowledge-base configuration

More information will be published with the August release in a new document on the solution’s product page (Pre-Production).

 

Variant Configuration service – Additional Filter Attribute for Field Knowledge-base Schema

The Knowledgebases API is extended with a new filter attribute kbSchema, allowing more precise filtering of runtime versions.

Additional improvements:

  • Correct handling of avcAlignedConfiguration
  • Consistent schema responses across knowledge-base generations

This enables better control and selection of knowledge bases in complex scenarios.

Example call:

new kbSchema filter

More information will be published with the August release in the API Definition and Development Guide (Pre‑Production).

 

Variant Configuration service – Class Node Specialization Improvement

An important correction has been implemented for class node specialization in scenarios involving characteristics referencing MARA-MATNR.

The class node specialization depends on the assigned values at product level. For characteristics with reference to MARA-MATNR there often seemed to be no local value assignment. Then, the "default" value assignment used to be set, which is too soft for class node specialization. This has been corrected by:

  • Assigning values correctly with author “classification”
  • Ensuring proper specialization behavior

This results in:

  • More accurate configurations
  • Improved consistency with back-end systems

 

Administration – Modern Administration User Interface

The administration UI is modernized with a new technology stack using React and UI5 Web Components.

Home Screen

Knowledge-bases Screen
Benefits include:

  • Faster and smoother UI rendering
  • Improved responsiveness
  • Enhanced support for asynchronous communication

This update significantly improves usability and lays the groundwork for future enhancements.

More information will be published in the Administration Guide (Pre‑Production) with the August release.

 

With that, you have a good overview of what innovations are planned for the 2608 release.

Please check the updated Roadmap and Release Notes after the release date to see which of these features were delivered as planned.

entry.title: Re: not able to activate connectivity service with connec...
entry.content:
Possible Causes

  • Wrong environment selected during service instance creation: The connectivity_proxy plan must be created at the subaccount level by selecting "Other" as the Runtime Environment and  not "Cloud Foundry". Creating it under Cloud Foundry will result in an error as mentioned in the Note 3289209 - Failed to create connectivity service instance with plan connectivity_proxy in BTP]
  • Multiple ServiceInstances or ServiceBindings created: In the Kyma environment, the Connectivity Proxy is provisioned only once into the kyma-system namespace. Creating more than one ServiceInstance or ServiceBinding causes inconsistencies and can prevent the proxy pod from activating correctly. [As mentioned in this blog]
  • ServiceInstance created via Kyma Dashboard instead of YAML: Creating the Connectivity Service via the Kyma Dashboard may not result in a connectivity proxy pod being created. Using the service YAML file is the recommended approach. [SAP Kyma using BTP Connectivity Service]
  • Cloud Connector Location ID mismatch: If the Cloud Connector has a Location ID configured but the application does not pass it correctly (via the SAP-Connectivity-SCC-Location_ID header or the CloudConnectorLocationId destination property), the connectivity proxy will fail to find the connector. [Connectivity-Proxy in KYMA not working]
  • Missing proxy configuration in the application: The application may not know the proxy address because the connectivity-proxy-info  ConfigMap and the XSUAA secret have not been mounted as volumes in the deployment.

Resolution Steps

  1. Create the service instance with the correct environment: In the SAP BTP cockpit, when creating a connectivity service instance with the connectivity_proxy plan, select "Other" (not "Cloud Foundry") as the Runtime Environment. For the service key, use "Create Service Binding" instead of the standard service key option.

  2. Ensure only one ServiceInstance and ServiceBinding exist: Verify that only one connectivity_proxy pod exists in the kyma-system namespace. Delete any duplicate ServiceInstances or ServiceBindings, as secret rotation caused by multiple bindings can cause proxy inconsistencies.

  3. Use the YAML-based approach for service creation: Instead of using the Kyma Dashboard, create the ServiceInstance and ServiceBinding using YAML files. Allow some time after creation for the Kyma reconciler (a scheduled job) to detect the resources and provision the proxy pod into kyma-system.

  4. Check and resolve Cloud Connector Location ID: If your Cloud Connector has a Location ID configured, ensure it is passed correctly via the HTTP header  SAP-Connectivity-SCC-Location_ID or the CloudConnectorLocationId destination property. If you only have one connector and no Location ID is needed, remove it from the Cloud Connector configuration.

  5. Mount the connectivity proxy ConfigMap and XSUAA secret into your deployment: Add the connectivity-proxy-info ConfigMap with the correct proxy host and ports, and mount it alongside the XSUAA secret as projected volumes in your application deployment. The proxy host should be set to connectivity-proxy.kyma-system.svc.cluster.local

    with port  20003 for HTTP.

  6. Check the Container Logs of the connectivity-proxy pod: If issues persist, inspect the logs of the connectivity-proxy-0 pod in the kyma-system namespace for detailed error information

  7. Ensure the ServiceInstance/ServiceBinding creation is not part of app deployment: This is a one-time setup and should be performed independently of any application deployment pipeline to avoid repeated secret rotation.

If it does not help please provide more details what are the exact steps which you performed and what is the error if you face any.

entry.title: SAP IDM: Read applications into IDM tables which have multi-value attribute(s)
entry.content:
In this blog-post, the example is of 'Reading Users from AD with the multi-value 'memberof' is considered. 

1. Pass type ' From LDAP  directory' 

2. Source tab: Ensure that memberOf is the attribute that is being read

3. Destination tab:  Lets say the main table is ZLDAP_READ.   In the target fields of this table, add a row such as Z_LDAP_MEMBEROF:GROUPS:NVARCHAR(500) with Source field as memberof. The datatype should be TABLE. Let's call this as child table.

Result: When the LDAP job/pass is executed, then the child table will contain all the group memberships for each user  while the main table will contain all other single-value attributes as configured in the Target/Source field.

If the Main and Child table is not existing then both get created during the 1st run  in the above set-up. If the Main table is already existing and the child table is added later , then the main table should be dropped . Re-run of the job will result in the creation/fill-up of both the tables

Note: Table Update to the Main table should be 'Add entries to table' , for users to be filled -in.

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: Suppress ODN for Consignment Issue Billing (CCIS/CCLF → F2)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are creating Sales Document Type CCIS with reference to Sales Document Type CCFU. At present, when a billing document is generated for CCIS, the system assigns Billing Type F2, and an ODN (Original Document Number) or invoice reference number is generated.

However, the business requirement is that ODN must not be generated for Sales Document Type CCIS when the Billing Type is F2, as this process is intended to be a simple mapping of consumption against the delivery challan.

Could you kindly help with a step by step solution to implement this validation accordingly?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: ODN must not be generated for & for Sales Document Ty...
entry.content:
Thank you for your suggestion.

However, we are using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (2-System Landscape), where this approach is not feasible due to standard Public Cloud restrictions.

Specifically:

  • Creating a custom billing type by copying the standard F2 billing type is not supported.
  • Creating or modifying copy control (for example, defining a new Delivery Type , Billing Type combination or creating new copy control entries) is also not supported. Customers can only use the copy control combinations delivered by SAP and make limited changes to existing supported entries.

Therefore, implementing a custom billing type without ODN assignment and linking it to CCIS is not a viable option in our landscape.

entry.title: [UTILITIES] New SAP Enable Now Simulations Added to IUT51 Explore SAP Market Communication for Util.
entry.content:
New SAP Enable Now Simulations Added to IUT51 Explore SAP Market Communication for Utilities in the German Energy Market 

We are pleased to announce that a comprehensive set of SAP Enable Now simulations has been added to the IUT51 learning portfolio, providing learners with hands-on guidance across key German utilities market communication and master data processes.

To access the simulations, please refer to the lesson https://learning.sap.com/courses/explore-sap-market-communication-for-utilities-in-the-german-energy-market/accessing-course-simulations-9.

Available Simulation Packages

IUT51 SAP Market Communication for Utilities – End-of-Supply

The End-of-Supply simulation package covers the complete process flow across supplier, distributor, and MaCo Cloud systems:

  • Business System Supplier – Searching for Point-of-Delivery in the Interaction Center
  • Business System Supplier – Starting Process End-of-Supply
  • MaCo Cloud Supplier – Monitoring Transfer Document with Business Data and Process Document with Message Data
  • MaCo Cloud Distributor – Monitoring Transfer Document with Business Data and Process Document with Message Data
  • Business System Distributor – Monitoring Process Document with Received Messages
  • Business System Supplier – Checking Point-of-Delivery and Supply Scenario

IUT51 SAP Market Communication for Utilities – Meter Reading Processing

Learners can follow the end-to-end meter reading process across market roles and system landscapes:

  • Business System Meter Operator (GUI) – Displaying Master Data and Entering Meter Reading Result and Sending Aggregated Message
  • Business System Meter Operator (Fiori) – Monitoring Transfer Document with Business Data
  • MaCo Cloud Meter Operator – Monitoring Transfer Document with Business Data and Process Document with Aggregated Message
  • MaCo Cloud Meter Operator – Monitoring Mass Meter Value Processes and Navigating to Individual Transfer Document
  • Business System Distributor (Fiori) – Monitoring Process Document with Received Message
  • Business System Distributor (GUI) – Checking Received Meter Reading Result

IUT51 SAP for Utilities – Master Data Germany

The Master Data Germany simulations provide detailed insights into the creation and display of master data for German market roles:

  • Business System Distributor (GUI) – Creating Master Data for all Market Roles (Standard Consumption Scenario with MMD and SLP)
  • Business System Distributor (GUI) – Displaying Master Data: Installation and MaLo with Supply Scenario
  • Business System Meter Operator (GUI) – Displaying Master Data: Installation and MeLo with Supply Scenario
  • Business System Meter Operator (Fiori) – Displaying MOS Billing Contract

These simulations complement the IUT51 learning content by allowing learners to explore realistic business scenarios and system interactions in a guided, self-paced environment. We encourage all learners, consultants, and partners working with SAP for Utilities in the German energy market to take advantage of these new hands-on learning resources.

entry.title: Re: Display Line Items in General Ledger App
entry.content:
Hello!

The balance app is designed for period totals by defined currency types (e.g., company code currency and parallel currencies). Document/transaction currency is inherently a line-item attribute and varies across postings for the same G/L account/period. Best practice alternatives for viewing document currency: Display Line Items in General Ledger: add the "Document Currency" and "Amount in Document Currency" columns, and filter/group as needed. Display Journal Entries:  search by G/L account, company code, period, etc., then show "Document Currency" and "Amount in DC." Manage Journal Entries: provides flexible filters and columns, including document currency at the line-item level.

entry.title: Using OpenTelemetry to monitor SAP Netweaver Java systems with SAP Focused Run System Monitoring
entry.content:
Instrumenting SAP NetWeaver Java AS with OpenTelemetry for SAP Focused Run

Introduction

System Monitoring capabilities in SAP Focused Run have reached a new milestone with support for the OpenTelemetry metrics format. This blog explores how to instrument your SAP NetWeaver Java AS with OpenTelemetry and seamlessly integrate it with SAP Focused Run's System Monitoring.

This represents the second installment of our "Instrumenting SAP NetWeaver Java AS with OpenTelemetry" series. If you haven't already, we recommend reviewing the first part to understand the foundational concepts.

Note: This guide assumes you have prior experience with SAP Focused Run applications, including System Monitoring and Agent Administration.

Prerequisites

Before diving into configuration, ensure you have the following in place:

  1. OpenTelemetry Java Agent

    • Download from https://opentelemetry.io/docs/zero-code/java/agent/getting-started/
    • Copy the agent file to your preferred directory
    • Example path: /usr/sap/<SID>/<InstNr>/otel/

  2. Simple Diagnostics Agent (SDA)

    • Minimum version: 1.68
    • Must be deployed on all managed system hosts

  3. Credentials

    • Valid sapadm user credentials

  4. Access & Privileges

    • Administrator privileges for NetWeaver Administrator (NWA)
    • Experience with SAP Focused Run System Monitoring

Configuration Steps in NetWeaver Administrator (NWA)

Step 1: Access NetWeaver Administrator

Open your web browser and navigate to:

http://<hostname>:<port>/nwa

You'll need administrator privileges to proceed.

Step 2: Navigate to Java System Properties

From the main menu:

  • Go to Configuration → Infrastructure → Java System Properties

Step 3: Select Your Java Server Node

  • Expand the template name
  • Choose the ID of the Java Server Node you wish to instrument

Step 4: Add the OpenTelemetry Java Agent

Under Additional VM Parameters, add the following line:

-javaagent:/<path_to_file>/opentelemetry-javaagent.jar

Replace <path_to_file> with your actual installation path.

Step 5: Configure System VM Parameters

Switch to the System VM Parameters tab and add the following parameters:

Parameter Name Parameter Value

otel.exporter.otlp.metrics.endpointhttp://<SDA host>:<SDA port>/lmsl/sda/default/v1/metrics
otel.exporter.otlp.protocolhttp/protobuf
otel.exporter.metrics.exporterotlp
otel.exporter.otlp.headers"Authorization=Basic c2FwYWRtOlBhc3N3b3Jk"
otel.service.name<instance name> (e.g., J00)

Important: The authorization header contains a base64-encoded credentials string in the format:

sapadm:<password>

Ensure you encode your actual sapadm password in base64 format.

Step 6: Repeat for All Java Application Instances

For each Java Application instance in your landscape, repeat Steps 3-5.

Configuration Steps on the SAP NetWeaver J2EE Host

Step 1: Install SDA

Deploy the minimum required SDA version (1.68) on your SAP NetWeaver J2EE host.

Step 2: Enable OpenTelemetry on SDA

Edit the SDA configuration file:

File Path: /usr/sap/hostctrl/SMDAgent/default/configuration/com.sap.solman.agent.mai.otel.v2/custom.properties

Add the following parameter:

otel.enabled=true

Note: In the initial version, the OTel data collector is switched off by default. This will be changed in the future SDA releases.

Step 3: Restart the J2EE System

Restart your J2EE system to activate OpenTelemetry data collection. The SDA will now begin receiving metrics from your system.

Configuration Steps in SAP Focused Run

Step 1: Access Template Maintenance or Metric Management

Create a custom metric in SAP Focused Run at the instance level using either:

  • Template Maintenance UI (System Monitoring), or
  • Metric Management (Custom Metric Management)

Step 2: Create a Metric Group

Begin by establishing a new metric group for your OpenTelemetry metrics.

Step 3: Select the OpenTelemetry Data Collector

In your metric group configuration:

  • Select the Data Provider (DP) named "OpenTelemetry Data Collector"
  • Ensure it's of type "Diagnostics Agent (push)"

Troubleshooting: If you can't locate the data collector, import the latest monitoring content version via Rapid Content Delivery.

Step 4: Configure Metric Names

For testing purposes, use .* as filter for the metric name parameter to display all collected metrics. Later, you can filter for specific metrics such as:

  • jvm.memory.used
  • Other JVM or application-specific metrics

Step 5: Assign Metrics to Alerts

Link your newly created metrics to alerts by either:

  • Assigning to existing alerts, or
  • Creating custom alerts as needed

Step 6: Distribute the Template

Save your changes and execute the "Apply & Activate" step to distribute the template across your monitored systems.

Step 7: Verify the results

The newly configured metrics can be found in the System Monitoring UI.

 

 

 

 

What's Next?

By following the guide, you're now prepared to consume the metrics in System Monitoring in OpenTelemetry format. As a next step we are going to release our SAP Focused Run/Cloud ALM-own OpenTelemetry extension in the near future. This extension will provide more relevant metrics, which can be used for monitoring and alerting. Once released, this blog page will be updated accordingly.

Have questions or run into issues? Share your experiences in the comments below and stay tuned for updates on OpenTelemetry integration in future SAP Focused Run releases.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public WM – How to group multiple p...
entry.content:
@Gabriel_Salomez

Yes, the Public Cloud supports the behaviour you described.

However, you must use RF picking with pick handling units, not ordinary warehouse task confirmation directly from each source bin to the final destination bin.

So, that process would be:

  • The operator receives one warehouse order containing the ten warehouse tasks.
  • The operator picks the ten tasks sequentially from the different source bins.
  • Each pick is confirmed into a pick handling unit assigned to the RF resource.
  • After the picking steps are completed, the pick handling unit is moved to the destination bin using the HU warehouse task.

See Confirm Product Warehouse Tasks for more details.

So, for your example:

Ten warehouse tasks with one destination bin: The operator can perform all ten picks during one trip and then place the pick HU at the destination.

Warehouse tasks with several destination bins: The operator can collect the goods using the appropriate pick HUs and perform one destination confirmation for each destination bin. Note that for multiple destination bins, each destination bin only needs to be scanned once during optimised HU placement.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/c186246ad5ce47a2830d19c6235ad0cd.html?locale=en-US

You can enable the process by:

  1. Use Control Display of Pick-HU Screen During RF Picking and configure the pick HU screen to be displayed during RF picking.
  2. Open Define Confirmation Methods for Pick-HUs and maintain the confirmation method required for optimised HU placement. This configuration is the prerequisite for the standard Placing Multiple Handling Units function.
  3. In RF, execute Pick by Warehouse Order, scan the warehouse order, create or scan the pick HU, and confirm the ten source picks sequentially.
  4. After you have completed the picks, continue to HU placement and confirm each destination bin once.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/bf4f52cb36164e39b31e4a71b41e162c.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/735bae8c309246a79e0e926c2374d1f0.html?locale=en-US

This will ensure the ten product warehouse tasks remain separately traceable, but the operator does not need to complete ten separate physical pick and drop trips. Make sure the pick HUs and the corresponding RF confirmation method are configured.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Moving HTML5 Applications to Application Frontend Service
entry.content:
This blog walks you through the changes you need to make when transitioning an MTA-based HTML5 application from the hosting services of HTML5 Repository and the managed approuter to the new Application Frontend service, and explains why the transition is worthwhile.

A Quick Recap: What Is Application Frontend?

Application Frontend is a SaaS application on SAP BTP for hosting, serving, and operating frontend applications. It was introduced as part of the SAP Build release and is designed to be a single point of entry for UI application management — bringing back the simplicity from the Neo HTML5 Runtime to the multi-cloud environment.

Check out this introduction blog for more details Introducing Application Frontend Service.

Key benefits of using Application Frontend

  • Versioning: Neo-style multi-version support with multiple active versions and a configurable default. You can test a new version in production without disrupting existing users.
  • Faster deployments: Deployments complete in seconds via CLI or API, ideal for fast dev test cycles.
  • Full application lifecycle control: Manage, monitor, and operate applications from the BTP Cockpit, CLI, or API — with observability and fine-grained control per application.
  • Simpler MTA setup: The destination-content module and all its service key scaffolding disappear — fewer modules, fewer service keys, and less configuration to maintain.
  • Visibility into used services: A “binding-like” behavior provides a clear view from the cockpit or CLI into the dependencies of your application.
  • And many other features that make your development experience easier.

Should you fancy using Application Frontend and like to move an existing application already deployed to the HTML5 Repository to Application Frontend, here are the steps to do it.

What Changes Are Needed?

Moving your application from HTML5 Repo to Application Frontend requires some changes in the application deployment and routing descriptors – the mta.yaml and xs-app.json.

The main change is in the mta.yaml which becomes much simpler - Application Frontend eliminates the need for instance-level destinations for storing credentials.

With HTML5 Repo, credentials for required services such as XSUAA, Workflow, and other services had to be exposed as destinations so the managed approuter could reach them. This required a dedicated module that created service keys and mapped them to instance-level destinations (module myApp-destination-content in the example below).

With Application Frontend, service credentials are stored directly within the application. You bind your services (XSUAA, Workflow, etc.) directly to the app-content module, and Application Frontend takes care of the rest.

The result: fewer modules, fewer service keys, and less configuration to maintain.

Note: the following example refers to an older work mode used with HTML5 repository service. If you already use the newer methodology where credentials destination is not used anymore, you can skip steps 1 and 2.

Your Current MTA Descriptor (html5-apps-repo)

Let's look at a sample MTA descriptor for deploying an HTML5 application. The application uses a destination to a backend service, an XSUAA instance, and the Workflow service.

_schema-version: "3.2"
ID: myApp
version: 0.0.1

modules:
  # Module that creates instance-level destinations with credentials
  - name: myApp-destination-content
    type: com.sap.application.content
    requires:
      - name: myApp-destination
        parameters:
          content-target: true
      - name: myApp-html-repo-host
        parameters:
          service-key:
            name: myApp-html-repo-host-key
      - name: myApp-xsuaa
        parameters:
          service-key:
            name: myApp-xsuaa-key
      - name: myApp-workflow-service
        parameters:
          service-key:
            name: myApp-workflow-key
    parameters:
      content:
        instance:
          destinations:
            # Destination with app-host credentials
            - Name: myApp-html-repo-host-destination
              ServiceInstanceName: myApp-html5-app-host-service
              ServiceKeyName: myApp-html-repo-host-key
              sap.cloud.service: business.solution.service
              Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
            # Destination with XSUAA credentials
            - Name: myApp-xsuaa-destination
              ServiceInstanceName: myApp-xsuaa-service
              ServiceKeyName: myApp-xsuaa-key
              sap.cloud.service: business.solution.service
            # Destination pointing to Workflow reuse service instance
            - Name: myApp-workflow-destination
              Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
              ServiceInstanceName: myApp-workflow-service
              ServiceKeyName: myApp-workflow-key
          existing_destinations_policy: ignore
    build-parameters:
      no-source: true

  # Module that deploys HTML5 application content
  - name: myApp-app-content
    type: com.sap.application.content
    path: .
    requires:
      - name: myApp-html5-repo-host
        parameters:
          content-target: true
    build-parameters:
      build-result: resources
      requires:
        - artifacts:
            - html5-app.zip
          name: html5-app
          target-path: resources/

  # HTML5 application module
  - name: html5-app
    type: html5
    path: html5-app
    build-parameters:
      build-result: dist
      builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm install
        - npm run build:cf
      supported-platforms: []

resources:
  # Destination service instance 
  - name: myApp-destination
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
        init_data:
          instance:
            destinations:
              # Destination to the backend service
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                Name: backend-api
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: https://backend-api.example.com
            existing_destinations_policy: update
        version: 1.0.0
      service: destination
      service-plan: lite

  # HTML5 Repo app-host service instance
  - name: myApp-html5-repo-host
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: html5-apps-repo
      service-plan: app-host

  # XSUAA service instance
  - name: myApp-xsuaa
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      path: ./xs-security.json
      service: xsuaa
      service-plan: application

  # Workflow reuse service instance
  - name: myApp-workflow-service
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: workflow
      service-plan: lite

parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo

Here are the needed Steps

1. Delete the Entire `myApp-destination-content` Module

With app-front, credentials for dependent services are stored directly with the application, so this destination is no longer needed.

Action: Remove the myApp-destination-content module completely.

2. Update the `myApp-app-content` Module

The app-content module now needs to:

  • Bind directly to the destination, XSUAA, and Workflow service instances (no longer via destinations)
  • Reference the app-front service instead of html-repo-host as the content target
  • Keep everything else unchanged (build parameters, artifact dependencies)

Current `myApp-app-content`:

requires:
  - name: myApp-html-repo-host
    parameters:
      content-target: true

Update to:

requires:
  - name: myApp-xsuaa
  - name: myApp-workflow
  - name: myApp-destination-service  
  - name: myApp-app-front
    parameters:
      content-target: true

3. Replace the `html5-repo-host` Resource

The HTML5 repository service (html5-apps-repo) is replaced with the Application Frontend service (app-front).

Current resource:

- name: myApp-html5-repo-host
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: html5-apps-repo
    service-plan: app-host

Replace with:

- name: myApp-app-front
  type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
  parameters:
    service: app-front
    service-plan: developer

4. Remove `HTML5Runtime_enabled` from Destination Service

The destination service is still needed to point to the backend, but the HTML5Runtime_enabled: true flag is no longer required.

Changed destination config:

config:
#  HTML5Runtime_enabled: true   ** This line is not needed anymore **
  init_data:
    instance:
      destinations:
        - Authentication: NoAuthentication
          Name: backend-api
          ProxyType: Internet
          Type: HTTP
          URL: https://backend-api.example.com
        existing_destinations_policy: update
  version: 1.0.0

5. Update xs-app.json

The xs-app.json file inside your HTML5 application controls how routes are handled. Routes that serve static content reference the hosting service by name — this needs to change from html5-apps-repo-rt to app-front.

Before:

{
  "source": "^/index.html$",
  "target": "/index.html",
  "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",
},
{
  "source": "^(.*)$",
  "target": "$1",
  "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt"
}

Update to:

{
  "source": "^/index.html$",
  "target": "/index.html",
  "service": "app-front",
},
{
  "source": "^(.*)$",
  "target": "$1",
  "service": "app-front"
}

Action: Replace every instance of "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt" with "service": "app-front". Routes pointing to backend destinations are unaffected.

That it!! No other changes are needed.

What Stays the Same

The following require no changes:

  • The HTML5 application module itself (html5-app) build commands, paths, etc.
  • XSUAA service instance configuration
  • Workflow service instance configuration
  • Backend destinations that do not carry a sap.cloud.service attribute

To Sum Up

Moving an existing UI application from HTML5 Repo to AppFront is a simple, straightforward process that will not impact the business logic of your application.

Note that the URL of the application will change, so bookmarks will have to be updated. If your application is integrated into an SAP Build Work Zone site, these settings will have to be updated as well.

The New MTA Descriptor (app-front)

For reference, here is the full descriptor after applying all required changes.

_schema-version: "3.2"
ID: myApp-id
version: 0.0.1

modules:
  # Module that deploys HTML5 application content
  - name: myApp-app-content
    type: com.sap.application.content
    path: .
    requires:
      - name: myApp-xsuaa
      - name: myApp-workflow
      - name: myApp-app-front
        parameters:
          content-target: true
    build-parameters:
      build-result: resources
      requires:
        - artifacts:
            - html5-app.zip
          name: html5-app
          target-path: resources/

  # HTML5 application module
  - name: html5-app
    type: html5
    path: html5-app
    build-parameters:
      build-result: dist
      builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm install
        - npm run build:cf
      supported-platforms: []

resources:
  - name: myApp-backend-destination
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        init_data:
          instance:
            destinations:
              # Backend destination without sap.cloud.service
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                Name: backend-api
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: https://backend-api.example.com
            existing_destinations_policy: update
        version: 1.0.0
      service: destination
      service-plan: lite

  # HTML5 app-front service instance
  - name: myApp-app-front
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: app-front
      service-plan: developer

  # XSUAA service instance
  - name: myApp-xsuaa
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      path: ./xs-security.json
      service: xsuaa
      service-plan: application

  # Workflow reuse service instance
  - name: myApp-workflow-service
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: workflow
      service-plan: lite

entry.title: SAP Continuous Influence Session for SAP S/4HANA Utilities - Third Anniversary Update
entry.content:
Three years ago already, we have launched the SAP Continuous Influence session for SAP S/4HANA Utilities as our single channel for collecting your suggestions for improvements related to SAP S/4HANA Utilities:

For background information, please refer to my previous blog post New SAP Continuous Influence session for SAP S/4HANA Utilities.

To mark this milestone, I'm pleased to share a status update on our progress.

Session Statistics (as of 1 July 2026)

In total we have 149 improvement requests (IRs) in our session.

51 IRs delivered – including 23 delivered during the last twelve months, , starting with those that have received most votes:

  • IR 351738 - Add Fields 'Market Location' and 'Metering Location' for App 'Resolve Implausible Meter Readings' 
  • IR 364511 - Request for Better Error Management in IS-U PF Process 
  • IR 357184 - §40 EnWG gesetzliche Verpflichtung zur aktiven Übermittlung einer Verbrauchsübersicht 
  • IR 357107 - Re-plausibilisation for later meter readings (ISU_DM_MR_FUTURE_PLAUSI active) 
  • IR 355596 - RTP-Schnittstelle mit Eingabevariable in Komponente / RTP-Interface with Input variable ... 
  • IR 354708 - Pre-filling of fields during the connection object search by address (Customer Engagement/ IC Agent) 
  • IR 354703 - Additional search options are needed in the free-text search of premise(Customer Engagement/IC Agent 
  • IR 330456 - Enterprise search for utilities objects 
  • IR 316432 - EDM: Customizing of profile version creation is not considered during settlement run 
  • IR 346592 - Fälligkeitsdatum beim Druckbeleg-Storno  
  • IR 373238 - Fiori IS-U Overview App based on a search (e.g Address, Customer, Point of Delivery, Installation) 
  • IR 317134 - EEDM02 transaction in Fiori 
  • IR 328753 - Deleting device info records en masse 
  • IR 345970 - Enhancement of Transaction EEDMPROFVALCHECK 
  • IR 346630 - Transaktion EEDMPROFVALCHECK Option “nur abrechnungsfähige Profilstatus“ 
  • IR 358288 - set due date of budget billing plan during invoicing 
  • IR 348044 - Profile Status and Status of Billing Orders do not correspond  
  • IR 352844 - Felder für Biller Direct sind in Customer Engagement nicht verfügbar 
  • IR 368454 - Allow meter reader (Ableser) in combination with meter reading type „Meter reading by the customer“ 
  • IR 331497 - MDUS Additional Attributes 
  • IR 346808 - Versorgungsanlage im Datenumfeld farblich differenzierbar Request 
  • IR 349323 - Expand utilities sales contract search with POD external ID 
  • IR 357685 - Analyzing Meter Reading Records with Reason Code 09 with Missing Gas Data 

For a complete list of all delivered improvement requests, visit: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3504/ideas-completed

3 IRs accepted and planned to be delivered in Q3–Q4 2026:

  • IR 363172 - Increase the maximum character limit for the “Code for Identifying a Register” 
  • IR 366770 - EG72 Device allocation additional start time and end time/ EG72 Gerätezuordnung zusätzl. Uhrzeit 
  • IR 356178 - Replacement of iMS (+smgw to mME)-Enablement of ami ws for assignment of base meter to MaLo/MeLo 

4 IRs marked as Planned (Long-Term) – identified as larger investments and included in the SAP S/4HANA Utilities product roadmap:

  • IR 330477 - Flexible Aggregation von Lastgängen / Flexible aggregation of load profiles 
  • IR 351296 - Fiori Billing and Metering Reading Overview/Analysis Apps 
  • IR 323237 - Public API for Developer Extensibility: RAP Interface for Utilities Contract Accounts /V... 
  • IR 352899 - SAP Fiori App for Contract Account Creation/Change/Display 

5  IRs under review.

19 IRs with Acknowledged status, including 5 with 7+ votes (approaching the 10-vote threshold for review)

2 IRs requiring additional information.

64 IRs not planned: Most rejected due to insufficient votes within 12 months or being outside the scope of utilities-specific functionality.

Important Reminders

This session focuses exclusively on utilities-industry specific features in SAP S/4HANA. For improvement requests related to other SAP S/4HANA topics, please see How Customers and Partners Can Give Input to the Roadmap of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition and the SAP Continuous Influence Sessions overview.

Please note that a valid S user is required to participate in our (and other) SAP Continuous Influence session(s). For details see knowledge base article 3014377 - Accessing the Continuous Influence Session "SAP S/4HANA Utilities". 

Thank You

We appreciate  for your active participation and engagement. Your input helps us to continuously improve SAP S/4HANA Utilities according to your business requirements.

entry.title: Consumption of CBO created in Customizing tenant by ABAP / Dev extensibility created CDS view?
entry.content:
Can a Custom Business Object created in the Customizing tenant (key user extensibility) be consumed by an ABAP CDS view created in the Development tenant / Eclipse (developer extensibility)?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: A report in S4 to see what's been invoiced from a Sales Order perspective and not invoiced.
entry.content:
Could you please confirm which SAP system version you are referring to in your request? Specifically, we need to know whether it is:

  • SAP S/4HANA (on-premise)
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud (public)
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud (private edition)

entry.title: Re: Consumption of CBO created in Customizing tenant by A...
entry.content:
No, unfortunately it is not possible. CDS views created via developer extensibility cannot consume custom business objects created using Key User tools in the Customizing tenant.

 

The documentation below outlines this:

Key User Extensibility | SAP Help Portal

"By default, key user extensibility objects can’t use developer extensibility objects and vice versa.

However, some key user development objects can use developer extensibility objects and vice versa. The following tables give you an overview of the applicable use cases:

Custom fields and data source extensions created with key user extensibility can be used by the following developer extensibility objects:
Key User Extensibility ObjectsDeveloper Extensibility ObjectsKey User App

Custom Fields/Data Source Extensions

  • ABAP Classes and Interfaces

  • CDS View Entities

  • CDS Custom Entities"

So while in some cases it is possible, this is not the case with Custom Business Objects created in the Customizing Tenant.

I hope this clarifies your question.

Kind regards,
Ronan Cullen

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Project Kernseife - improving the measurement of Clean Core in ABAP
entry.content:
@LBretschneider 

Thanks for the introducing this Project Kernsiefe for clean core analysis.
We have implemented  and are using it to perform Clean Core analysis for our SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud system. 

During our review of the analysis results, we observed that objects of type ENHO (Enhancement Implementations) e.g.  Implicit Enhancements , Explicit Enhancements , BAdI Implementations are not included in the scan scope. 

This raises a concern because Implicit Enhancements are typically regarded Clean Core Level D. Since ENHO objects are excluded, these enhancements and the custom code contained within them do not seem to be evaluated or reflected in the analysis results.

Could you please clarify:

  1. Is the exclusion of ENHO objects from the Kernsiefe scan scope an intended design decision?
  2. If yes, how are enhancement implementations (especially implicit enhancements and BAdI implementations) considered within the Clean Core assessment?
  3. Is there any configuration, enhancement, or roadmap item that would allow ENHO objects to be included in the scan and reporting scope?
  4. What is SAP's recommended approach to identify and assess enhancement-based custom code that is currently not covered by the Kernsiefe analysis

entry.title: Requirement to Exclude Student Business Partners from SAP DQM Address Validation Based on BP Number
entry.content:
We are using SAP Data Quality Management (DQM) Microservices for Address Validation in our SAP S/4HANA system. Currently, address validation is being executed for all Business Partners created or maintained in S/4HANA, including Business Partners migrated from external systems such as Ariba and other third-party applications.

We have a business requirement to exclude Student Business Partners from address validation.

Current scenario:

  • Student Business Partners are created and migrated from the Banner system.
  • Student Business Partners are maintained using a dedicated BP grouping/number range in SAP S/4HANA.
  • Address validation through SAP DQM is currently triggered for these Student Business Partners as well.

Requirement:
We would like to determine whether SAP DQM Microservices Address Validation can be configured or enhanced to skip address validation for Student Business Partners based on:

  • BP Number Range
  • BP Grouping
  • BP Type
  • BP Role
  • Any other supported Business Partner attribute

entry.title: SAP MDG AI - Joule Capabilities Overview & Configuration
entry.content:
Overview:

SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) is increasingly enhanced with generative AI and Joule (SAP’s AI copilot) to simplify master data creation, maintenance, and governance. These AI capabilities reduce manual effort, improve data quality, and allow business users to perform complex MDG tasks using natural language without deep technical knowledge.

Key benefits include:

  • Natural language processing (NLP) for creating and updating master data change requests.
  • AI-generated summaries of changes for faster review and approval.
  • Intelligent assistance for Business Partners, Materials, Finance, and custom objects.
  • Faster governance processes with proactive insights and automation.

These features are primarily available in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS0 and higher (especially Cloud Private Edition / Central Governance).

Main Discovery Center Entry:

AI Feature - Joule Capabilities for Master Data Governance

Capabilities:

  • Joule Capabilities for Master Data Governance. Use conversational AI to create, update, and review master data via natural language prompts.
  • AI-Assisted Change Requests: Generate change requests from plain English descriptions.
  • AI-Generated Summaries: Automatically summarize changes in a change request for quicker governance and approval.
  • Support for Multiple Domains: Strong support for Business Partner (BP), with expanding support for materials, finance, and custom/flex entities.
  • Official Help Link: Joule Capabilities for Master Data Governance
  • This video describes new Joule Capabilities in MDG on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, such as creating and editing Business Partner data.
  • 3634627 - Joule Capabilities for Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Cloud

Components:

  • CA-MDG-CMP-JOULE (Joule Content for Consolidation and Mass Processing in MDG)

SAP Notes (Important references):

  • 3634627 - Joule Capabilities for Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Cloud
  • 3562330 - MDG: Implementation guide for AI-assisted change for custom entities - SAP for Me
  • 3573659 - AI-assisted change request in Master Data Governance, Central Governance for S/4HANA Cloud
  • 3526419 - MDG-AF: Supported entities and attributes for AI-assisted change - SAP for Me

Configuration and Prerequisites:

  • Minimum Required Version: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS0 (Feature Pack Stack 0) or higher.
  • Activation of relevant Joule scenarios and AI services (via SAP BTP / Generative AI Hub where applicable).
  • Proper user authorizations for MDG apps and Joule.
  • For custom entities: Follow implementation guides in the relevant SAP Notes.
  • Review Master data governance Joule capabilities for a list of prerequisites.  

AI-Specific Features and resources in SAP MDG:

  • AI-Assisted Features: New AI-Assisted Feature in SAP Master Data Governance
  • Discovery Center AI Feature Page: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, master data governance
  • Community Blogs (great for overviews and examples):
    • SAP Document AI in MDG via ISLM
    • Search SAP Community for "MDG AI" or "Joule MDG" for more recent posts

Product roadmap:

  • View the latest planned innovations in the Roadmap for MDG and AI: Road map Explorer
  • What's New in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, master data governance​ - Release 2025 Pages 19-24 include Joule AI co-pilot for SAP Master data governance.

Additional Useful Links:

  • Joule Setup Guide: Comprehensive Setup Blog
  • MDG Standard Authorization Objects & Roles: Authorization Objects Used by MDG, Central Governance

entry.title: MD01/02/03 Default setting and check values
entry.content:
I have seen that there are a few questions about the same theme....

Sorry. 

But I have the requirement that the default values in different SAP-Transaction (Old Gui :-)) shout be changed. 

A few of the values are hardcoded in e. G. MM61XO20_INIT_D0150 in MODULE INIT_D0150 OUTPUT: 
    MOVE CHAR1   TO RM61X-DISER.

I have also read that there ist Transaction SHD0. But this way doesn't work by me. 
Also is a way to say the user - bevore you work - pleas save the settings in the transaciton (beause the user can save them in the Menu)...
But this is not a secure way....

Is there a way to make an enhancement to set the default values? I think that that is also cracy - how I am sure I have realized it in every Transaktion where I need them...

There is also the requirement - how cann we check - that the user has not changed the input-values. Or can we change the input-fields to only output, that the user can´t change them...

A Alternative solution can be to write the table UMD01 (Default Values for mrp). Than use an Enhancement way if there is an update / write bei User-IDOC. 

If it is necessary - we have S4Hana on Premise - SAP S/4HANA 2023. 

I am very curious to see what possible solutions exist and which ones other have chosen / preffered.

entry.title: Re: Existe forma de registrar no DRC(Public) notas histór...
entry.content:
Hello!

Question 1: This capability exists in SAP's product line, but is not available in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The feature is called "Upload documents" (transaction NFE_CLOUD_UPLOAD_DOC, report NFE_CLOUD_UPLOAD_DOCUMENT), delivered via SAP Note 3004205. Per SAP's own documentation: "You can use the transaction, for example, when you want to upload a document previously issued on another source system or application for a branch that was already migrated to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil." But SAP Note 3004205 targets SAP_APPL and S4CORE releases using the on-premise/Private Edition versions. SAP KBA 2941021 (about the same J1BNFE/NF-e-CT-e Monitor area) lists its applicable products as SAP ERP 6.0, SAP S/4HANA, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, not "SAP S/4HANA Cloud".

Question 2: Does "Upload Electronic Documents to eDocument Cockpit" (DC_NFe) apply to this scenario? This feature belongs to the inbound invoicing option for Brazil (for receiving supplier/B2G documents), a different process.

Recommendation:

  • There is currently no standard, documented, and verified-available mechanism in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to load pre-authorized legacy outbound NF-e/CT-e/NFS-e into the DRC monitor for audit-only visibility.
  • Treat this as an archival/documentation requirement rather than a DRC/eDocument-framework requirement: keep the legacy system's authorized XMLs and DANFEs in a dedicated external repository or archiving solution, accessible to auditors independently of the DRC monitor.
  • Raise an incident with SAP explicitly asking whether a Public Edition equivalent of NFE_CLOUD_UPLOAD_DOC exists or is planned.

 

SAP links:

SAP Note 3004205 – Report to upload files to cloud - outbound invoicing option for Brazil

SAP KBA 2941021 – Nota Fiscal document appearing as "In process" in J1BNFE

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0864cb07010642b3bde45a20de4975bc/5c7715efa3c8439999928320d8431160.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SLH_NFe?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SLH_NFe/71b3b178e5cb4a50a88cf2985a14f17c/f39ffd4f80654de281ae3a235416c6cb.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/doc/0a466fa353ac4c81b3d20bfe22d68f26/Cloud/en-US/FSD_SAP_Document_Compliance_Outbound_Brazil_EN.pdf

entry.title: How to Assign Authorizations for Analytics for Universal Journal
entry.content:
Managing access to financial data requires careful attention to user roles and permissions. In SAP's universal journal, different accountants need different views of the same data—and that's where authorizations come into play.

What Is the Universal Journal?

The universal journal serves as the foundation for integrated accounting. It combines financial and management accounting data into a single chart of accounts, eliminating the need for reconciliation between these areas. This unified approach brings together:

- General Ledger Accounting
- Asset Accounting
- Controlling
- Profitability Analysis (integrated with margin analysis)
- Material Ledger

All data is stored in table ACDOCA, creating one source of truth for your financial information.

Why Authorizations Matter

Even though everyone accesses the same underlying data, business requirements differ by role. A general ledger accountant might need visibility into all company code data for financial statements, while a cost accountant should only see specific cost centers relevant to their area.

Setting Up Access: Business Roles and Business Catalogs

You grant access by assigning business roles to users using the Maintain Business Roles app. These roles combine pre-defined business catalogs with restriction types that control read and write access at the field level. 

Learn how to set up access: Maintain Business Roles

Key Business Roles and Catalogs

Here are some common examples:

- General Ledger Accountant: SAP_FIN_BC_GL* (Template: SAP_BR_GL_ACCOUNTANT)
- Asset Accountant: SAP_FIN_BC_AA* (Template: SAP_BR_AA_ACCOUNTANT)
- Cost Accountant - Overhead: SAP_FIN_BC_OH* (Template: SAP_BR_OVERHEAD_ACCOUNTANT)
- Cost Accountant - Sales: SAP_FIN_BC_SA* (Template: SAP_BR_SALES_ACCOUNTANT)
- Cost Accountant - Inventory: SAP_FIN_BC_IA* (Template: SAP_BR_INVENTORY_ACCOUNTANT)
- Cost Accountant - Production: SAP_FIN_BC_IA* (Template: SAP_BR_PRODN_ACCOUNTANT)

The beauty of this system is its flexibility. You can use the provided templates as starting points or create custom business roles tailored to your organization's specific needs. 

Setting Up Access: Restriction Types

Figure: Restriction Types in the System

 
To further define access to customer data, authorization values are assigned to restriction fields, which are grouped into restriction types. General organizational restriction types are found in the General section of the Maintain Business Roles app and each contains a single restriction field. The combined settings across all restriction types determine the overall authorization granted to the business role and its assigned users.

Configure restriction types consistently across all relevant business catalogs to ensure users can work effectively within their applications.

Learn how to use restriction types: How to Define Authorizations Based on Restrictions

For a detailed list of the restriction types for analytics for the universal journal, see Authorizations for Analytics for Universal Journal .

Further Reading:

Universal Journal
Business Catalogs
Business Role Templates

entry.title: Custom Widget in SAC not recognizing files.
